justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Strong signals explicitly acknowledged — observability, load testing, circuit breakers, and deployment strategy are called out as production-grade in high_confidence_areas. (2) Non-idempotent write + retry = guaranteed duplicates identified as the critical blocker, with detailed explanation of the mechanism (lost ACK → retry → duplicate row). (3) DLQ with no consumer explicitly called 'silent data loss masquerading as fault tolerance.' (4) At-least-once + non-idempotent = data quality issue under normal operation clearly stated ('not an edge case — it is a guaranteed data corruption path'). (5) Specific recommendations provided: idempotency key/dedup key on warehouse writes, DLQ consumer with alerting on depth > 0. (6) Verdict is NOT READY, grounded in data correctness issues. (7) Pippen does not let strong observability compensate — explicitly separates 'production-grade for the happy path' from the data integrity failures.

observations.text: Exceptionally clear articulation of why at-least-once + non-idempotent writes = guaranteed duplicates under normal operation, not just edge cases
DLQ issue framed precisely as 'silent data loss masquerading as fault tolerance' — captures the deceptive nature of the pattern
Quantified the blast radius: 0.1% duplicate rate at 10K events/sec = ~864K duplicate rows/day, making the problem concrete
Correctly identified that retroactive deduplication grows harder over time, adding urgency to pre-deployment fix
Strong separation between operational strengths (observability, deployment, circuit breakers) and data correctness failures — no conflation
Escalations are well-structured with clear routing and actionable minimum requirements
